target.title: fix(gateway): log hook rejection diagnostics
target.body:
## Summary

- Add compact, sanitized warning logs for authenticated Gateway hook JSON rejection paths.
- Preserve existing hook HTTP statuses, response bodies, auth behavior, mapping behavior, and dispatch behavior.
- Keep the fix scoped to `src/gateway/server/hooks-request-handler.ts` plus focused hook-handler tests and harness injection.

Fixes #69192.

## AI assistance disclosure

This PR was prepared with Codex assistance. I reviewed and understand the affected Gateway hook request path, including `src/gateway/server/hooks-request-handler.ts`, `src/gateway/hooks.ts`, `src/infra/http-body.ts`, and the focused tests.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Gateway hook requests that return expected JSON 4xx rejections now emit one sanitized warning line with stable diagnostic fields, so operators can diagnose body-read, direct validation, and mapped validation failures from the Gateway log/journal.

- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw Gateway runtime on loopback from this source checkout at `62e51975d74b`, with throwaway `OPENCLAW_HOME`, `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`; throwaway hook token; no real credentials or external integrations.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  ```bash
  # Proof harness wrote .artifacts/issue-69192-proof-revised.33BA0D/state/openclaw.json
  # with gateway auth disabled, hooks enabled at /hooks, and one mapped agent hook.
  OPENCLAW_HOME=.artifacts/issue-69192-proof-revised.33BA0D/home \
  OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=.artifacts/issue-69192-proof-revised.33BA0D/state \
  OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=.artifacts/issue-69192-proof-revised.33BA0D/state/openclaw.json \
  OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL=debug \
  OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 \
  pnpm openclaw gateway --port 38035 --bind loopback --allow-unconfigured

  # Then the harness sent three authenticated POST requests to:
  # http://127.0.0.1:38035/hooks/wake              with invalid JSON
  # http://127.0.0.1:38035/hooks/agent             with invalid channel payload
  # http://127.0.0.1:38035/hooks/mapped-message    with scalar JSON payload
  # The throwaway Authorization token value is intentionally omitted from the PR body.
  ```

- Evidence after fix:

  Copied live output and redacted runtime log excerpt from the isolated Gateway run:

  ```text
  proof_dir=.artifacts/issue-69192-proof-revised.33BA0D
  commit=62e51975d74b
  port=38035
  invalid_json status=400 body={"ok":false,"error":"Unexpected token 'o', \"not-json\" is not valid JSON"}
  direct_validation status=400 body={"ok":false,"error":"channel must be last"}
  mapped_scalar status=400 body={"ok":false,"error":"hook mapping requires message"}
  hook_rejection_logs:
  2026-05-25T16:24:12.146-04:00 [hooks] hook rejected path=wake code=400 reason=body-invalid-json method=POST content-type=application/json body-type=unparsed body-bytes=8 request-id=proof-invalid-json
  2026-05-25T16:24:12.161-04:00 [hooks] hook rejected path=agent code=400 reason=agent-channel-invalid method=POST content-type=application/json body-type=object body-bytes=54 request-id=proof-direct-validation
  2026-05-25T16:24:12.165-04:00 [hooks] hook rejected path=mapped-message code=400 reason=mapping-validation method=POST content-type=application/json body-type=string body-bytes=22 request-id=proof-mapped-scalar
  log_leak_check=PASS
  status_checks=PASS
  ```

- Observed result after fix: The three representative hook rejection responses stayed at 400 with the existing response bodies, and each emitted a journal-visible `[hooks] hook rejected ...` warning with path, status code, stable reason, method, content type, body type, bounded body length, and request id. The warning lines did not contain the throwaway hook token or sensitive payload strings.

- What was not tested: Live third-party webhook senders, successful hook dispatch into an agent run, external channel delivery, and auth/method/query-token/text-response rejection branches. The changed surface is authenticated JSON rejection diagnostics, and the proof intentionally avoided real credentials and live integrations.

- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Proof used loopback Gateway runtime and throwaway state/config only. It did not use real hook credentials or live external integrations.

## Root Cause

The hook request handler returned expected JSON 4xx responses from several authenticated branches without a corresponding rejection log. Body-read failures, direct `wake`/`agent` validation failures, mapped hook validation failures, and policy/session-key failures therefore had user-visible failures but weak operator diagnostics.

## Dependency contract

`src/gateway/hooks.ts` wraps `src/infra/http-body.ts`. The body reader returns stable limit codes and current response strings:

- `PAYLOAD_TOO_LARGE` -> `payload too large`
- `REQUEST_BODY_TIMEOUT` -> `request body timeout`
- `CONNECTION_CLOSED` -> `Connection closed`
- invalid JSON -> parser error text

The patch maps these existing strings to stable hook rejection reasons without changing the response body or status mapping.

`src/logging/subsystem.ts` accepts `logHooks.warn(message, meta)`. Because console/journal logs surface the message most directly, the patch puts sanitized key/value diagnostics in the warning message and does not log raw request bodies or authorization material.

## Regression Test Plan

- `src/gateway/server-http.hooks-request-timeout.test.ts` now covers warning logs for invalid JSON, direct validation rejection, and mapped validation rejection.
- The tests assert stable reason codes, request ids, body types, bounded body length, and absence of fake tokens/raw payload strings in warning messages.
- `src/gateway/server-http.test-harness.ts` now lets focused tests inject `logHooks` and hook config without starting a real Gateway server.

## Security Impact

Positive. The new logs improve diagnosability without logging Authorization headers, hook tokens, raw request bodies, rendered hook messages, channel targets, or session keys. Header-derived log values are normalized to one line and length bounded. Body length is reported from `content-length` only and capped.

## Human Verification

I reviewed the affected hook ingress path, the body reader contract, the logging contract, and the final narrowed diff. I also reviewed Codex Spark's implementation handoff and trimmed the patch to remove the earlier helper module plus mapping-contract/test changes.

## CODEOWNERS / owner review

`.github/CODEOWNERS` only has secops ownership for gateway auth/secret/security-specific files, and none of the final touched paths match those patterns. Gateway maintainer review is still recommended because this changes hook ingress logging semantics.

## Changelog

No `CHANGELOG.md` edit. This is a contributor PR; maintainer/AI landing should add the release changelog entry if accepted.

## Review conversations

- Checked issue #69192 and exact open/closed PR searches.
- No open PR linked to or substantially addressing #69192 was found.
- Reviewed closed PR #69267 as prior art; it was closed unmerged and had broader/unrelated changes.
- Ran `codex review --base origin/main`; final run reported no discrete correctness regression.

## Risks

- The patch intentionally adds one warning log for each covered hook JSON 4xx rejection, which may increase warning volume for misconfigured or noisy webhook senders.
- Stable reason codes are now an operator-facing diagnostic surface; future hook rejection branches should use the same helper and focused coverage when they join this path.

## Behavior tradeoffs

- The warning message favors compact grep-friendly key/value fields over verbose prose so it is useful in plain journal/console output.
- Invalid JSON response bodies still include parser text as before, but the new warning line intentionally does not include that parser text to avoid echoing raw body snippets into logs.
- Mapped hook validation failures use the generic `mapping-validation` reason to avoid changing the mapping helper contract just for logging.

## Scope boundaries

- Does not change hook auth behavior, method/query-token handling, final hook 404 handling, status codes, response bodies, mapping selection, dispatch behavior, channel delivery, or session-key policy.
- Does not add a new config option, dependency, generated file, or public mapping contract.
- Does not log text-response rejection branches or broaden hook payload logging beyond sanitized metadata.

## Validation

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-http.hooks-request-timeout.test.ts
OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree pnpm check:changed
codex review --base origin/main
```

Focused tests: `src/gateway/server-http.hooks-request-timeout.test.ts` passed, 8 tests.

`pnpm check:changed`: guard lanes and `typecheck core` passed; `typecheck core tests` failed on latest-main baseline errors in `src/agents/agent-command.live-model-switch.test.ts` and `src/tui/embedded-backend.test.ts`. I confirmed the same four errors on detached `origin/main` with:

```bash
git switch --detach origin/main
OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test-main-baseline-final.tsbuildinfo
git switch codex/issue-69192
```

Final `codex review --base origin/main`: no discrete correctness regression.

Real Gateway behavior proof: passed; see the Real behavior proof section above.
target.author: ferminquant
